2026-04-27T07:24:37.8601979Z ##[group]Run # Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)
2026-04-27T07:24:37.8603152Z [36;1m# Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)[0m
2026-04-27T07:24:37.8603602Z [36;1m# Mask immediately to prevent timing vulnerabilities[0m
2026-04-27T07:24:37.8603994Z [36;1mAPI_KEY=$(openssl rand -base64 45 | tr -d '/+=')[0m
2026-04-27T07:24:37.8604310Z [36;1mecho "::add-mask::${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-27T07:24:37.8604549Z [36;1m[0m

2026-04-27T07:25:47.3410038Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-27T07:25:31.886337947Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":58688,\"title\":\"[Mgmt Generator] Backward-compat ModelFactory overloads call modern overload with wrong positional argument order (CS1739)\",\"body\":\"## Bug Description\\n\\nWhen the mgmt generator emits `[EditorBrowsable(Never)]` backward-compatibility overloads for a `ModelFactory` method, the **call site inside the back-compat overload** uses positional arguments in the order of the model\\u0026#39;\\u0026#39;s fields rather than the order of the modern factory\\u0026#39;\\u0026#39;s parameter list. The result is a malformed call that fails to compile with `CS1739: The best overload for \\u0026#39;\\u0026#39;X\\u0026#39;\\u0026#39; does not have a parameter named \\u0026#39;\\u0026#39;Y\\u0026#39;\\u0026#39;`, because the compiler can\\u0026#39;\\u0026#39;t resolve the call to the modern overload at all.\\n\\n## Reproduction\\n\\n`Azure.ResourceManager.Redis` (PR https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/pull/56877) — generated `Generated/ArmRedisModelFactory.cs`.\\n\\n### Modern overload (line 489) — parameter order: `zones, location, tags, identity, redisConfiguration, ...`\\n\\n```csharp\\npublic static RedisCreateOrUpdateContent RedisCreateOrUpdateContent(\\n    IEnumerable zones = default, AzureLocation location = default,\\n    IDictionary tags = default, ManagedServiceIdentity identity = default,\\n    RedisCommonConfiguration redisConfiguration = default, string redisVersion = default,\\n    bool? enableNonSslPort = default, ..., bool? isAccessKeyAuthenticationDisabled = default,\\n    ZonalAllocationPolicy? zonalAllocationPolicy = default, RedisSku sku = default,\\n    ResourceIdentifier subnetId = default, IPAddress staticIP = default)\\n{ ... }\\n```\\n\\n### Back-compat overload (line 675) — emitted as:\\n\\n```csharp\\n[EditorBrowsable(EditorBrowsableState.Never)]\\npublic static RedisCreateOrUpdateContent RedisCreateOrUpdateContent(\\n    IEnumerable zones, AzureLocation location, IDictionary tags,\\n    ManagedServiceIdentity identity, RedisCommonConfiguration redisConfiguration,\\n    string redisVersion, bool? enableNonSslPort, ..., bool? isAccessKeyAuthenticationDisabled,\\n    RedisSku sku, ResourceIdentifier subnetId, IPAddress staticIP)\\n{\\n    // BUG: positional args in MODEL FIELD ORDER, not modern-factory PARAM ORDER\\n    return RedisCreateOrUpdateContent(\\n        redisConfiguration,           // ← modern factory expects `zones` (IEnumerable) here\\n        redisVersion,                 // ← modern factory expects `location`\\n        enableNonSslPort,\\n        replicasPerMaster,\\n        replicasPerPrimary,\\n        tenantSettings,\\n        shardCount,\\n        minimumTlsVersion,\\n        publicNetworkAccess,\\n        updateChannel,\\n        isAccessKeyAuthenticationDisabled,\\n        zonalAllocationPolicy: default,    // ← named, but compiler resolves to a different overload that lacks this param\\n        sku,\\n        subnetId,\\n        staticIP,\\n        zones,\\n        location,\\n        tags,\\n        identity);\\n}\\n```\\n\\n### Compiler error\\n\\n```\\nerror CS1739: The best overload for \\u0026#39;\\u0026#39;RedisCreateOrUpdateContent\\u0026#39;\\u0026#39; does not have a parameter named \\u0026#39;\\u0026#39;zonalAllocationPolicy\\u0026#39;\\u0026#39;\\nerror CS1739: The best overload for \\u0026#39;\\u0026#39;RedisCreateOrUpdateContent\\u0026#39;\\u0026#39; does not have a parameter named \\u0026#39;\\u0026#39;isAccessKeyAuthenticationDisabled\\u0026#39;\\u0026#39;\\n```\\n\\nThe same pattern occurs at line 738 (a second back-compat overload missing `isAccessKeyAuthenticationDisabled`). The same broken pattern also exists for `RedisData` back-compat overloads in the same file.\\n\\n## Root Cause Hypothesis\\n\\nThe generator\\u0026#39;\\u0026#39;s back-compat-overload emitter appears to:\\n1. Take the param list of the contract (baseline) factory method (which had the original property/field order)\\n2. Emit the call to the modern factory using those parameter **names** as positional arguments\\n\\n…but it does not reorder them to match the modern factory\\u0026#39;\\u0026#39;s actual parameter order. With safe-flatten/`@flattenProperty` reorderings between baseline and modern, the resulting call site is invalid.\\n\\n## Workaround\\n\\nHand-rolled back-compat overloads in `Customization/ArmRedisModelFactory.cs` that delegate to the modern factory using **named arguments** (which are order-independent) instead of relying on the generator-emitted positional call.\\n\\n## Environment\\n\\n- **Mgmt emitter**: `@azure-typespec/http-client-csharp-mgmt@1.0.0-alpha.20260423.3`\\n- **Affected SDK**: `Azure.ResourceManager.Redis` (PR #56877)\\n- **Related**: #57334 (modern factory drops flat params), #57755 (back-compat overload `global::.TypeName`), #52667 (client+safe flatten)\",\"state\":\"open\",\"html_url\":\"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/58688\",\"user\":{\"login\":\"haiyuazhang\",\"id\":1924967,\"profile_url\":\"https://github.com/haiyuazhang\",\"avatar_url\":\"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/1924967?v=4\"},\"author_association\":\"MEMBER\",\"labels\":[\"Mgmt\",\"CodeGen\"],\"reactions\":{\"total_count\":1,\"+1\":0,\"-1\":0,\"laugh\":0,\"confused\":0,\"heart\":0,\"hooray\":0,\"rocket\":0,\"eyes\":1},\"created_at\":\"2026-04-27T07:22:54Z\",\"updated_at\":\"2026-04-27T07:22:54Z\"}","type":"text"}]}}}
2026-04-27T07:25:47.3429333Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-27T07:25:31.898986975Z","direction":"OUT","type":"REQUEST","server_id":"github","method":"tools/call","payload":{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"tools/call","params":{"arguments":{"issue_number":58688,"method":"get","owner":"Azure","repo":"azure-sdk-for-net"},"name":"issue_read"}}}
2026-04-27T07:25:47.3453693Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-27T07:25:32.141344775Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":58688,\"title\":\"[Mgmt Generator] Backward-compat ModelFactory overloads call modern overload with wrong positional argument order (CS1739)\",\"body\":\"## Bug Description\\n\\nWhen the mgmt generator emits `[EditorBrowsable(Never)]` backward-compatibility overloads for a `ModelFactory` method, the **call site inside the back-compat overload** uses positional arguments in the order of the model\\u0026#39;\\u0026#39;s fields rather than the order of the modern factory\\u0026#39;\\u0026#39;s parameter list. The result is a malformed call that fails to compile with `CS1739: The best overload for \\u0026#39;\\u0026#39;X\\u0026#39;\\u0026#39; does not have a parameter named \\u0026#39;\\u0026#39;Y\\u0026#39;\\u0026#39;`, because the compiler can\\u0026#39;\\u0026#39;t resolve the call to the modern overload at all.\\n\\n## Reproduction\\n\\n`Azure.ResourceManager.Redis` (PR https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/pull/56877) — generated `Generated/ArmRedisModelFactory.cs`.\\n\\n### Modern overload (line 489) — parameter order: `zones, location, tags, identity, redisConfiguration, ...`\\n\\n```csharp\\npublic static RedisCreateOrUpdateContent RedisCreateOrUpdateContent(\\n    IEnumerable zones = default, AzureLocation location = default,\\n    IDictionary tags = default, ManagedServiceIdentity identity = default,\\n    RedisCommonConfiguration redisConfiguration = default, string redisVersion = default,\\n    bool? enableNonSslPort = default, ..., bool? isAccessKeyAuthenticationDisabled = default,\\n    ZonalAllocationPolicy? zonalAllocationPolicy = default, RedisSku sku = default,\\n    ResourceIdentifier subnetId = default, IPAddress staticIP = default)\\n{ ... }\\n```\\n\\n### Back-compat overload (line 675) — emitted as:\\n\\n```csharp\\n[EditorBrowsable(EditorBrowsableState.Never)]\\npublic static RedisCreateOrUpdateContent RedisCreateOrUpdateContent(\\n    IEnumerable zones, AzureLocation location, IDictionary tags,\\n    ManagedServiceIdentity identity, RedisCommonConfiguration redisConfiguration,\\n    string redisVersion, bool? enableNonSslPort, ..., bool? isAccessKeyAuthenticationDisabled,\\n    RedisSku sku, ResourceIdentifier subnetId, IPAddress staticIP)\\n{\\n    // BUG: positional args in MODEL FIELD ORDER, not modern-factory PARAM ORDER\\n    return RedisCreateOrUpdateContent(\\n        redisConfiguration,           // ← modern factory expects `zones` (IEnumerable) here\\n        redisVersion,                 // ← modern factory expects `location`\\n        enableNonSslPort,\\n        replicasPerMaster,\\n        replicasPerPrimary,\\n        tenantSettings,\\n        shardCount,\\n        minimumTlsVersion,\\n        publicNetworkAccess,\\n        updateChannel,\\n        isAccessKeyAuthenticationDisabled,\\n        zonalAllocationPolicy: default,    // ← named, but compiler resolves to a different overload that lacks this param\\n        sku,\\n        subnetId,\\n        staticIP,\\n        zones,\\n        location,\\n        tags,\\n        identity);\\n}\\n```\\n\\n### Compiler error\\n\\n```\\nerror CS1739: The best overload for \\u0026#39;\\u0026#39;RedisCreateOrUpdateContent\\u0026#39;\\u0026#39; does not have a parameter named \\u0026#39;\\u0026#39;zonalAllocationPolicy\\u0026#39;\\u0026#39;\\nerror CS1739: The best overload for \\u0026#39;\\u0026#39;RedisCreateOrUpdateContent\\u0026#39;\\u0026#39; does not have a parameter named \\u0026#39;\\u0026#39;isAccessKeyAuthenticationDisabled\\u0026#39;\\u0026#39;\\n```\\n\\nThe same pattern occurs at line 738 (a second back-compat overload missing `isAccessKeyAuthenticationDisabled`). The same broken pattern also exists for `RedisData` back-compat overloads in the same file.\\n\\n## Root Cause Hypothesis\\n\\nThe generator\\u0026#39;\\u0026#39;s back-compat-overload emitter appears to:\\n1. Take the param list of the contract (baseline) factory method (which had the original property/field order)\\n2. Emit the call to the modern factory using those parameter **names** as positional arguments\\n\\n…but it does not reorder them to match the modern factory\\u0026#39;\\u0026#39;s actual parameter order. With safe-flatten/`@flattenProperty` reorderings between baseline and modern, the resulting call site is invalid.\\n\\n## Workaround\\n\\nHand-rolled back-compat overloads in `Customization/ArmRedisModelFactory.cs` that delegate to the modern factory using **named arguments** (which are order-independent) instead of relying on the generator-emitted positional call.\\n\\n## Environment\\n\\n- **Mgmt emitter**: `@azure-typespec/http-client-csharp-mgmt@1.0.0-alpha.20260423.3`\\n- **Affected SDK**: `Azure.ResourceManager.Redis` (PR #56877)\\n- **Related**: #57334 (modern factory drops flat params), #57755 (back-compat overload `global::.TypeName`), #52667 (client+safe flatten)\",\"state\":\"open\",\"html_url\":\"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/58688\",\"user\":{\"login\":\"haiyuazhang\",\"id\":1924967,\"profile_url\":\"https://github.com/haiyuazhang\",\"avatar_url\":\"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/1924967?v=4\"},\"author_association\":\"MEMBER\",\"labels\":[\"Mgmt\",\"CodeGen\"],\"reactions\":{\"total_count\":1,\"+1\":0,\"-1\":0,\"laugh\":0,\"confused\":0,\"heart\":0,\"hooray\":0,\"rocket\":0,\"eyes\":1},\"created_at\":\"2026-04-27T07:22:54Z\",\"updated_at\":\"2026-04-27T07:22:54Z\"}","type":"text"}]}}}
